mapping.a_element: extremization of a surface functional (the defect extremal surface / island prescription that extremizes a generalized entropy)
mapping.b_element: extremization of a cost functional via the Pontryagin Minimum Principle

mapping.a_element: quantity being computed by extremization: fine-grained entanglement entropy
mapping.b_element: quantity being minimized by the optimal control: final size of the infection

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic notion of a variational/extremization principle: paper A geometrically extremizes an area/generalized-entropy functional (RT/defect-extremal-surface plus the island formula in a holographic AdS/ICFT dual) to obtain entanglement entropy, whereas paper B applies the Pontryagin Minimum Principle to a constrained ODE control problem to minimize epidemic final size. The governing machinery differs entirely — curved-spacetime holographic surface extremization on an end-of-the-world brane versus Hamiltonian optimal-control of SI dynamics under an integral supply constraint — so no coherent role-for-role mapping of the actual mechanisms holds.
